content.claim: Early tracking is associated with increased inequality of opportunity across OECD countries; meta-analytical evidence shows tracking increased the social achievement gap (families with few vs. many books) by 6.90 score points (effect size d = 0.44)
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Effect on mean/average performance is contested across studies
Cross-national correlation does not establish causation within single countries
Meta-analysis aggregates across different assessment instruments and time periods
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
